forecasts.reasoning: The question asks if Muqtada al-Sadr's movement announced participation in the 2025 Iraqi parliamentary elections *before July 1, 2025*. Extensive reporting from July, September, and October 2025 confirms that he remained committed to a boycott throughout the first half of the year. On July 10, 2025, Shafaq News reported that al-Sadr 'remains committed to boycotting Iraq’s upcoming parliamentary elections'. Furthermore, in July 2025, an article from September 2025 notes that Sadr explicitly cut ties with 31 members of his movement who 'defied his call to boycott the electoral process'. While there are reports that he was 'reportedly persuaded' to return as the November 2025 elections approached, this clearly occurred after the July 1, 2025 deadline. Because he actively enforced and remained committed to his boycott through the deadline date, the probability that an announcement to participate occurred *before* July 1, 2025 is functionally zero.
